Shiny Brass Idol  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
EXPENDABLE Charges: 1
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
Effect: Divine Aura (Any Slot, Casting Time: Instant) as Level 20
WT: 0.4 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Shiny Brass Idol
Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
EXPENDABLE Charges: 1
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
Effect: Divine Aura (Any Slot, Casting Time: Instant) as Level 20
WT: 0.4 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Potion
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:2 pp 4 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:27
Submitted By:Hathor
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-19 01:45:02
Page Updated:Sun Sep 26th, 2021

Expansion: Original
Average Price: 170,000pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 20
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Highpass Hold
NPC Name
Hagnis Shralok
Vopuk Shralok


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Highpass Hold
Be warned, this is EXPENDABLE and will disappear when used, even if it has an augmentation in it.

For more invulnerability clickies, see this wiki article.

You obviously havnt tried this.

Sure the mage gets down safely.

But.. he cant COTH anyone down into the hole as it is a different zone to up top from where you jump.

#REDACTED, Posted: Apr 09 2004 at 1:26 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) There is no lev in the Hole. I found out the hard way once upon a time.
application

# Feb 19 2004 at 10:38 AM Rating: Default

64 posts
this item would make the NPC that Druids and Rangers need to talk to in the Hole easier to get to... hand it to a mage and have them use the entrance that causes instant death... then CoTH everyone else down...

RE: application
QuoteReply
# Aug 07 2004 at 11:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591787492985
Scholar
139 posts
Yup - this is my favorite way to raid the hole. I grab one of these sweeties before the raid, pop it on, jump in the Hole and start summoning the raid.
▲ Page top
RE: application
QuoteReply
# Mar 26 2004 at 12:58 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965790794
328 posts
No offense, seriously wondering...but why would a mage need a DA item to jump in the Hole? Summon an instant click lev ring and the minute you are done zoning, activate the ring and you are fine.

Or am I missing something - like no lev in The Hole (it's been so long since I did anything there).

I know bard lev takes too long to activate, as well as anyone elses cast lev. But the lev rings are insta-cast - I thought, anyway.
▲ Page top
RE: application
QuoteReply
# Apr 09 2004 at 1:20 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1600042698492
Scholar
51 posts
There is no lev in the Hole. I found out the hard way once upon a time.
▲ Page top
RE: application
QuoteReply
# Nov 16 2004 at 8:46 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591815562908
6 posts
***** all this lev and DA talk. You don't need a single "item" to survive the fall. You zone in, look down and see the floor coming at you, "/q" a split second before you hit. You go LD, your NPC self you instantly created does not take the falling damage, you log back in alive and well.

I've done this a number of times. Course it would be considered an exploit I'm sure, so I wouldn't recommend the entire raid force knowing about it and doing it all at once. Might look a little suspicious. Just do it once with the mage, and CoTH the rest down.
▲ Page top
RE: application
QuoteReply
# Nov 16 2004 at 1:54 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Hmm, you have done this several times? And CoTHed the raid down after. So how did you get back into the group, in order to CoTH them down.

As soon as you /q ed out, you lost your group, so even if the fall didn't kill you, you're alone, ungrouped at the bottom of the hole, with no way to get a group invite, and therefore no way to CoTH anyone down.

Care to elaborate?
▲ Page top
RE: application
QuoteReply
# Feb 01 2005 at 1:47 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592261621619
28 posts
Could have a cleric in group DA down the hole with you. Log back in, group invite from cleric, voila. Just have to make sure cleric survives any mobs that might be in the room while you're gone.

Edited, Tue Feb 1 13:49:15 2005
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 04 2003 at 8:10 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) was just passin through High Hold pass with my chanter, tryin to get my pet spell, and this guy started attackin me. casted anarchy (was only spell i had memmed at the time) and owned him pretty quickly, he droped this. i guess i got real luckey haha everyone of the noobs were askin me about the pack...
Geez
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2003 at 3:43 PM Rating: Default

Man, when I used to camp Highpass like 3 years ago, we used to argue who had to take the idol and the halberd :) Only thing anyone ever wanted was the pack. Wish I hadn't used it up just to see what it did...

#REDACTED, Posted: May 27 2003 at 11:03 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) i have one for sale on prexussend tell to padas im selling for 1k
i'm a twit!
QuoteReply
# Mar 22 2003 at 2:50 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591784651258
21 posts
i had one of these...

i got it in a trade, a very good trade the more i learn about the items i got.

well, neither of us knew what the effect of divine aura was...

i held onto it for a while, then when i was setting up in the bazaar one day i checked the price on it... it was around 1k... the prices ranged from 800 to 1.1k, so i set mine to 750 i think.

anyways, before i had gone afk for the night this bad boy had been sold.

it has been a while since i played my cleric, and now that i have looked up what the effect is i remember how many times that invuln spell saved my ***... wish i had kept it and bought some mana batteries... oh well...

so, if you get one, don't be so quick to sell it.

i guess the only other information that can be gleemed from this post is pricing data...

on the karana server these sell for around 1k give or take 100plat...

good luck everyone

Edited, Sat Mar 22 14:21:45 2003
▲ Page top
RE: i'm a twit!
QuoteReply
# Nov 21 2003 at 7:07 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592256695338
View User Forum
54 posts
It's not rechargeable and it disapears after you use it so it is a one shot deal.

You were probably better of selling it and use the plat to upgade your gear.
▲ Page top
does anyone know
QuoteReply
# Sep 16 2002 at 9:25 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592246212392
24 posts
say i cast this on myself, after porting into PoH, and before i ported in i dueled to die....
i get what i wanna pick up inside, cast this on myself to not get killed...can i still get a ressurection while this effect is up? even more..can you be ressed while fighting? just hit yes and poof outta the fight?
▲ Page top
RE: does anyone know
QuoteReply
# Aug 30 2004 at 4:25 PM Rating: Decent
Valdon
674 posts
Id have to say yes to the res/fight/idol question. The reason is you can act in all normal ways, you just cant fight or be hit. You can not be hurt from falls or anyhting. A great place to use it,in the same zone, is when getting the testament of vaneer. You fight the guy, and go to loot, sometimes that 50+ something smuggler jumps you, you click it... and run off the ledge to the 3 rogues and zone into the keep.

There was a change however. The second version of this spell for a cleric was changed for a while. It took quite a bit longer to cast, and had a damage shield. It was around this time THIS version of the spell was altered also. The mobs now recognize they can not hit you, and will basically flee from you. It used to be they would stay on you and keep trying to hit you.

They went ahead and changed the second version again, this time to a invulnerable and a heal over time - HOT. This original version, staying somewhat altered.

So its kind of changed. The mobs dont FLEE but rather run out, like when someone else grabs the agro. The problem is, if they grab say the warrior, you can not cast while the effect is on.
____________________________
· 'Valdon Gravedancer' Terris-Thule. Dwarven Cleric 34th circle.
· 'Junglebug Sugarpants' Terris-Thule. Halfling Druid. 52nd season.
· 'Shinook Eldergod' Terris-Thule. Troll Shaman of 32nd rite.
· 'Soultree Vixen' Terris-Thule. Woodelf Ranger of 34th season.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 10 2002 at 4:54 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I have one forsale on Rodcet Nife, send a tell to Ianluil or Oblaze and we can make a deal.
Time until you're out when you /q?
QuoteReply
# Jul 16 2002 at 3:59 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591773916956
View User Forum
Sage
164 posts
Since this has turned into a discussion of the effectiveness of Divine Aura, I figured I'd post my question while we're on the subject.

From reading these posts, I understand that you can't fight or cast while the DA effects are active, but can you camp or /q?

When raids turn ugly and the clerics need to get out, it seems like this idol with its instacast version of Divine Aura might be perfect for clerics who need the extra 18 seconds to get out.

Since it's a cleric spell, I figure a wise cleric should be able to tell me if you can camp or /q after casting Divine Aura. If so, I will give the Idol I've been saving since level 18 to the epic cleric the next time we do CT.

Truthfully, I would've destroyed it long ago except I thought it was my Goranga Idol. The icon is the same.
▲ Page top
RE: Time until you're out when you /q?
QuoteReply
# Aug 27 2002 at 6:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592254289310
4 posts
well 18 seconds is 18 seconds even if your LD. If you decide to /q when you have 17 seconds left, you have 17 seconds of invulnerbility.

as for camping out, sure you can - fopr the first 18 seconds. after that if a mob or AE hits you, you automaticly stand up again. Dont forget you cannot cast while having DA on, no harmshield, no divine barrior, etc and you cant cast when you are sitting.

the only real way that i can think of in which a cleric really needs to get out but is in the middle of a firefight is if he/she cast DA, sits down and camps, right when it ends (and you have to time this perfectly) a bard cast group DA and the cleric is protected for another 18 seconds.
▲ Page top
Fear raids
QuoteReply
# Apr 22 2002 at 11:29 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592758242409
12 posts
this item is invaluable for fear reaids. you get to player each with and idol and they can pull the intire castle with sow and than kite while groups peel off mobs and kill at will. These are worth 900pp each for a fear raid easy

__DEL__1591771363187

7 posts
Is the idol insta-cast or is there a casting time?

RE: Cast delay on idol?
QuoteReply
# Jun 20 2003 at 2:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591780436448
Scholar
23 posts
Upon right-clicking in an open inventory slot, the SBI gives the glug-glug sound (like drinking water, maybe 1/2 second?) and then the DA icon comes up.
▲ Page top
Insane!
QuoteReply
# Sep 27 2001 at 9:41 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592250056329
71 posts
People on Veeshan are insane. I got one of these and now I'm hoping to sell it for 100-200pp. The funny thing is NO ONE WANTS TO HAGGLE! I start off with a high price and they say 'That's ok' or 'You're crazy!' Last guy I got to haggle offered me 25pp for it saying that it was not worth that much. Do people not realize that this is a common drop from a VERY VERY RARE SPAWN?!? I camped the Orcs in Highpass for FOUR DAYS and only saw 3 of the named orcs. Shiny Brass Halberd dropped twice and this dropped once. If this thing is so worthless why don't you see them all over the place? Those that have the means and the patience to camp stuff like this should expect to set thier price for thier time and effort.
▲ Page top
RE: Insane!
QuoteReply
# Sep 03 2002 at 10:46 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770852477
20 posts
This is a common drop from a rare spawn? hmm.. good luck then that the group my 17 wizzy was is got the halbred and this to drop, oh well, gave it to a 19 ranger, hope she makes good use of it,

Gthulin 18 wizzy on Zeb (PS, sorry to the person on zeb looking for this)
Asede 36 ranger on zeb (dying is so fun!)
▲ Page top
RE: Insane!
QuoteReply
# Oct 26 2001 at 10:38 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
You need to auction in high level zones. To a level 20, this seems fairly worthless, becuase dying is common, and doesn't cost you that much. However, for a lvl 50+ who likes to solo, or goes on raids, this item could be priceless. If you're a caster, and overnuke, this gives the tanks time to peel (and actually decreases your hate, according to a recent patch message), or are soloing a necro,or mage, and pet dies too soon, and you've overpulled, (or tryiong to solo any other class, for that matter) <click> time to get to zone. Considering the cost of a 96% exp rez, or even worse, a corpse run to the planes, this item is certainly worth several hundred plat.
▲ Page top
RE: Insane!
QuoteReply
# Feb 16 2002 at 3:42 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1618791888885
Scholar
122 posts
Necros have Harmshield, which is their own exact version of DA, although this item would save a spell slot and has instant cast.

Unfortunately, by the time death really becomes painful and therefore ideal for this item, you're rarely hunting within 18 seconds of a zone break. Although I've had Harmshield for over 30 levels now, it's my experience that it simply gets me 18 seconds closer to the zone before I die.
▲ Page top
RE: Insane!
QuoteReply
# Sep 03 2002 at 10:51 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591770852477
20 posts
its also 18 seconds to get to a better place to die or if you run when you have low hp, its 18 seconds of that MOB not smacking your back as you running like hell or evacing out (does evac even work if you have this? i don'yt mean if you try to cast evac, but if it is cast on you?) anyways, necros have ways of getting out of danger besides this too, FD, gate, shadow step, and some others ones, not to metion your pet whihc can add several seconds onto your life if a kite or something goes sour, found that out with my chanter, still died but was closer to neriak in a area i knew, anyways, this is good item to get

blah blah blah, read other post for toons and levels
▲ Page top
RE: Insane!
QuoteReply
# Sep 10 2002 at 5:48 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Evac will not work while DA is up. No spells, beneficial, or harmful, can be cast on you while DA is going. As far as being more than 18 seconds from the zone, it may be true that this won't save you very time, but it will save you SOME of the time. Particularly, I've noticed that as you run to the zone from deep in some dungeons, the pathing will often help you get away, as mobs will peel for no reason, and take the long way. I HAVE survived before, due to DA.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 12 2001 at 4:40 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) well, you're a fool, DA, in most cases, only stalls your death for 18 seconds.
RE: Insane!
QuoteReply
# Dec 09 2001 at 3:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591772065779
8 posts
18 seconds can be a lifetime when your in the blender with 4 reds and your within eyesight of the zone or gaurds...
▲ Page top
RE: Insane!
QuoteReply
# Nov 21 2003 at 7:22 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592256695338
View User Forum
54 posts
Lets see with 18 seconds of invulnerability I have been able to escape escape from Velks (jump from the top shelf is a major rush), Permafrost (Vox Raid), Seb (trained from zone in to first floor zone out), KC (warlord room), and several camps in guk. DA/DB is very handy even if it is only 18 seconds. I would bet this item is used more for pulling difficult mobs rather then escaping difficult pulls. Most monks feel a lot better about pulling ubah_mob_x in ubah_loot_zone-y when they have one of these. They can FD and /q out when DA is up.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 17 2001 at 6:16 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) just picked one up tonight if anyone wants it ...for sale on saryrn...make an offer plz 8)
Idol Price
QuoteReply
# Sep 01 2001 at 11:54 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766850186
16 posts
I have found that highbies will pay a crazy amount for this item in Gfay. I had one and thought it only went for 20 plat, i saw a highbie auction'ing to buy the item and i sent a tell asking how much he was willing to pay. He Said 700 plat. With a big grin i ran to him and made a good profit. Since then i've been farming 'em.

So far i've sold em for 700p 700p 900p and 500p. If you find the right buyer, they'll pay.

Claudaria Shadowheart
31st Shadow Knight

Liliania Whispp
30th Druid
Xegony Server

hehe
QuoteReply
# Aug 30 2001 at 8:51 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591766669120
1 post
ok im one of those dum schmucks who values others almost as much as himself and rareley runs if he is in a good group and if im in a bad group i dont usualy stick around long enough to get attacked but anyway i like this item for the unsual times.
anyway i havent played for a while so dont quote me on this but i have noticed you dont get rewarded at all for saving a group member while i once helped someone in Eastcommons and got some fairly nice armour for it basicaly dont take your group members for granted or they may turn in to @$$ holes

Haso mage of the 34th circle beliver of quellious on the tribunal
▲ Page top
what is divine aura
QuoteReply
# Aug 03 2001 at 7:03 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591764914594
12 posts
what dos divine aura do??
▲ Page top
RE: what is divine aura
QuoteReply
# Aug 24 2001 at 6:37 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591765272889
4 posts
Divine Aura is a cleric spell that gives about 10 seconds of Invulnerability. You can't cast or fight while under its effects, but it is handy for making a quick run for it.
▲ Page top
RE: what is divine aura
QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2003 at 4:40 PM Rating: Default
Glasshoppa
Scholar
77 posts
Actually it is 3 ticks so 18 seconds.
